title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Go Client Systems · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A platform SDK team integrating a staging control plane must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for http caching, freshness, validators, vary, and invalidation, producing spec-aware cache decision notebook. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go 1.26 net/http spec-aware cache decision notebook while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Private Shared Cache
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client stores a response with max-age=60, ETag "rev-6", Age:6, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Private Shared Cache.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Private Shared Cache, keep Private Shared Cache connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

steps.title: Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Stale Revalidation Invalidation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client stores a response with max-age=20, ETag "rev-2", Age:2, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

steps.title: Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Stale Revalidation Invalidation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client stores a response with max-age=40, ETag "rev-4", Age:4, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Stale Revalidation Invalidation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Stale Revalidation Invalidation, keep Stale Revalidation Invalidation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure.
